rulings.Case Title: JULIE LEE VS. STEVE LY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Reopen Case
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 28, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JULIE LEE's Motion To Reopen Case. The motion is denied. Plaintiff's motion fails to comply with CCP 473. The motion is untimely and plaintiff does not demonstrate mistake, inadvertence, or excusable neglect. The court notes that a dismissal does not necessarily prevent a party from seeking to enforce a CCP 664.6 settlement. The Legislature amended CCP 664.6 in 1993 and a party can now seek to enforce a CCP 664.6 settlement despite the existence of a dismissal. See Wackeen v. Malis, (2002) 97 Cal.App.4th 429, 439. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: GUERNEVILLE BUSINESS CORPORATION, INC. A VS. ALBERT L BOASBERG
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 28, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANTs ALBERT BOASBERG, SCOTT MANDELL, ARTHUR FONDEN's Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings. The motion for judgment on the pleadings is denied in its entirety. The Second Amended Complaint is not facially time barred and defendants do not sufficiently address plaintiff's tolling and discovery allegations. Plaintiff states a cause of action for Breach of Duty of Good Faith and Fair Dealing. The implied covenant is implied in every contract. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges that Fonden breached the insurance adjustment agreement and that Mandell breached the settlement agreement. Even if plaintiff cannot obtain tort recovery on this cause of action, plaintiff can recover contractual damages. Plaintiff states a cause of action for Fraud and Deceit. Plaintiff is alleging a deceit/concealment claim that does not require the same amount of particularity as an intentional misrepresentation cause of action. Plaintiff alleges the Breach of Settlement Agreement cause of action. Plaintiff alleges that Mr. Mandell should not have been acting for plaintiff under the agreement. Plaintiff alleges that Breach of Fiduciary Duty cause of action. A disputed issue remains regarding whether Mr. Boasberg was acting properly when he was acting as plaintiff's attorney. Plaintiff states a claim for Intentional Interference with Contract. Paragraph 160 of the complaint alleges that defendants had knowledge of the Mandell settlement. Plaintiff's other charging allegations sufficiently plead the claim. Plaintiff states a claim for common law conversion. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: D.L. FALK CONSTRUCTION INC. VS. SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, A et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Disco
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 28, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT SAN FRANCISCO UNIFIED SCHOOL DISTRICT, Motion To Compel Further Responses To Discovery. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. The tentative ruling as to interrogatories and requests for production is to grant in part and deny in part. The Court further intends in its discretion to modify some of the specific discovery requests to resolve objections. The defendant is directed to produce for review at the hearing the documents received referenced in the plaintiff's responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NICK C GRAY et al VS. MARGARET L NARRON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Joinder In Co-Defendnat Margaret L. Narron'S Spceial Motion To Strike Pltffs Verified Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, October 28, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT STEPHEN NARRON's Joinder In Co-Defendnat Margaret L. Narron's Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Verified Complaint. Granted. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the Defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity¿If the court finds such a showing has been made¿it then determines whether the Plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) The first prong is met, as the malicious prosecution claim arises from protected activity. (See Daniels v. Robbin (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 204, 215, "The plain language of the anti-SLAPP statute dictates that every claim of malicious prosecution is a cause of action arising from protected activity because every such claim necessarily depends upon written and oral statements in a prior judicial proceeding.") The burden thus shifts to Plaintiffs to establish a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs "must demonstrate that the complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a sufficient prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment." (Premier Med. Mgmt. Systems, Inc. v. Cal. Ins. Guar. Ass'n (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 464, 476.) The elements of malicious prosecution are: (1) a favorable determination on the merits of the underlying action, (2) which was brought without probable cause, and (3) which was initiated with malice. (Contemporary Services Corp. v. Staff Pro Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1043, 1046.) "It is hornbook law that the plaintiff in a malicious prosecution action must plead and prove that the prior judicial proceeding of which he complains terminated in his favor." (Casa Herrera, Inc. v. Beydoun (2004) 32 Cal.4th 336, 341.) Plaintiffs have not pled or proven that CUD-14-647497 (the "Second Unlawful Detainer"), the case on which Plaintiffs' malicious prosecution claim is based, has terminated. A minute order dated February 20, 2014 in the Second Unlawful Detainer states that the Court sustained a demurrer and motion to strike with leave to amend. Although the ruling is reflected in the minutes, an Order was never filed. As such, the time for the plaintiff to amend in the Second Unlawful Detainer has yet to commence. (See CCP sec. 472b.) Further, no request for dismissal has been filed, nor has a judgment been entered. Plaintiffs therefore cannot plead or prove that the Second Unlawful Detainer terminated in their favor. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
